skill.name: imagen
skill.description: Generate images using Google Gemini's AI image generation model during Claude Code sessions. Creates visuals for frontend UIs, documentation, and creative projects.
skill.summary: AI-powered image generation skill using Google Gemini
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is a documentation file (SKILL.md) describing an image generation tool. All static findings are false positives: (1) Markdown code fences were misidentified as Ruby backtick execution, (2) The GitHub source URL is legitimate metadata, (3) Documenting GEMINI_API_KEY as an environment variable is the correct secure practice. No actual security risks identified.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Misidentified
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged markdown code fences (```) as Ruby/shell backticks. The file is SKILL.md documentation, not executable Ruby code. Python command examples in code blocks are legitimate documentation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: These are clearly markdown code fences showing Python command examples, not backtick execution. The file extension is .md, not .rb.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Hardcoded Source URL
dangerous_patterns.description: The flagged URL at line 4 is the skill's source GitHub repository URL - standard metadata for skill documentation, not a suspicious network request.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Source URL is expected metadata in skill files. It points to a legitimate GitHub repository for skill attribution.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Environment Variable Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: Documenting GEMINI_API_KEY as an environment variable is the correct secure practice. The skill does not hardcode secrets - it instructs users to provide their own API key.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Using environment variables for API keys is the recommended secure approach. The skill correctly documents this requirement rather than hardcoding credentials.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
dangerous_patterns.description: No cryptographic code exists in this file. Lines flagged contain only documentation text and YAML frontmatter.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Manual inspection confirms no cryptographic algorithms are present. The scanner appears to have matched on unrelated text patterns.

content.value_statement: Create stunning images directly in your Claude Code workflow using Google Gemini AI. Perfect for frontend development, documentation visuals, and creative projects without leaving your terminal.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using Google Gemini Pro Image model
Save generated images to custom file paths and directories
Specify image output size (supports options like 2K resolution)
Create images for UI components, documentation, and visual content
Works cross-platform on Windows, macOS, and Linux
limitations: Requires a Google Gemini API key to function
Depends on Gemini API availability and rate limits

use_cases.title: Frontend Developer Hero Images
use_cases.description: Quickly generate hero images and visual assets for landing pages and web applications without needing external design tools.

use_cases.title: Documentation Illustration
use_cases.description: Create diagrams and illustrations for README files, technical documentation, and project wikis.
use_cases.target_user: Technical writers and developers documenting projects

use_cases.title: UI Asset Creation
use_cases.description: Generate placeholder images, icons, and UI assets for component development and prototyping.
use_cases.target_user: UI/UX designers and developers creating mockups

prompt_templates.title: Basic Image Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of [describe your image concept] and save it to ./generated/image.png
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a simple image created from a text description

prompt_templates.title: High Resolution Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a high resolution (2K) image of [describe image] and save it to [path]
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a high-quality image for print or large displays

prompt_templates.title: UI Component Asset
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a [type: icon/avatar/placeholder] image for a [describe component context] and save it to [desired path]
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating specific UI assets for web or mobile applications

prompt_templates.title: Documentation Diagram
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a [style: diagram/illustration/chart] showing [concept to visualize] for technical documentation and save to [path]
prompt_templates.scenario: Visualizing architectures, flows, or concepts for documentation

output_examples.input: python scripts/generate_image.py "A futuristic city skyline at sunset"
python scripts/generate_image.py --size 2K "Minimalist app icon for music player" "./assets/icons/music.png"
output_examples.output:
High resolution image generated!
Saved to: ./assets/icons/music.png

best_practices: Set your GEMINI_API_KEY environment variable before running the skill
Use descriptive, detailed prompts for better image results
Specify output paths that match your project structure
anti_patterns: Do not share your GEMINI_API_KEY in code or public repositories
Avoid generating extremely large images as they may timeout
Do not rely on this for critical production assets without backup plans

faq.question: How do I get a Gemini API key?
faq.answer: Visit the Google AI Studio or Google Cloud Console to obtain a Gemini API key. Set it as the GEMINI_API_KEY environment variable before using the skill.

faq.question: What image formats are supported?
faq.answer: Currently only PNG format is supported for generated images.

faq.question: Can I generate multiple images at once?
faq.answer: The skill processes one image at a time. For batch generation, you would need to run the command multiple times or modify the script.

faq.question: Is this skill free to use?
faq.answer: The skill itself is free, but you pay for Google Gemini API usage based on your API key's associated billing account.

faq.question: Why is my image generation failing?
faq.answer: Common causes include: invalid or missing GEMINI_API_KEY, API rate limits, network issues, or prompts that violate Gemini's content policies.

faq.question: Can I use this for commercial projects?
faq.answer: Yes, generated images can be used in commercial projects, but check Gemini's terms of service for any restrictions on generated content usage.
